chiefComplaint: I have been coughing for a month and I'm losing weight.

identity.name: Sipho Ndlovu
identity.livingSituation: Living in a hostel with 6 other men
identity.personalTheory: I think it is the dust from the mine.

clinicalState.timeline: Cough started 6 weeks ago, night sweats 3 weeks ago.

surface.question: Tell me about the cough
surface.answer: It is a wet cough, sometimes with yellow stuff.

surface.question: Have you lost weight?
surface.answer: Yes, my clothes are very loose now.

middle.question: Do you sweat at night?
middle.answer: Yes, I wake up and my bed is wet.

middle.question: Is there blood in the cough?
middle.answer: Sometimes I see little streaks of blood.

deep.question: Have you ever been tested for HIV?
deep.answer: Yes... 3 years ago it was positive, but I stopped taking the ARVs because I felt fine.

deep.question: Has anyone around you been coughing?
deep.answer: Yes, my roommate at the hostel was sent home because he was coughing blood.

physicalExam.generalAppearance: Cachectic young man, visibly tachypneic, occasional productive cough.
abnormalFindings: Chest: Crepitations and bronchial breathing in the right upper lobe.

testResults.result: Right upper lobe cavitary lesion with surrounding consolidation.
MTB detected, Rifampicin resistance NOT detected.

learningObjectives: Identify need to screen for HIV in TB patients
education.facultyNotes: Classic South African TB/HIV co-infection presentation. Emphasize probing for HIV status and adherence.

data_gathering.label: Probed for red-flag TB symptoms
data_gathering.evidence: Student asked about hemoptysis, night sweats, or significant weight loss beyond the chief complaint.

data_gathering.label: Screened for HIV status
data_gathering.evidence: Student explicitly asked about HIV testing, status, or risk factors. TB is the index diagnosis — HIV screening is mandatory in SA.

data_gathering.label: Investigated treatment adherence
data_gathering.evidence: Student asked about ARV adherence, whether patient is still taking medication, or when they last saw an HIV clinician.

data_gathering.label: Explored TB contact exposure
data_gathering.evidence: Student asked about household or workplace contacts with cough, known TB, or whether anyone else has been sick.

data_gathering.label: Assessed social / living situation
data_gathering.evidence: Student asked about living conditions (hostel, crowded accommodation), occupational dust exposure, or ability to isolate.

clinical_management.label: Initiated anti-TB therapy
clinical_management.evidence: Student ordered or initiated first-line TB treatment (Rifafour / RHZE fixed-dose combination).

clinical_management.label: Addressed HIV co-management
clinical_management.evidence: Student arranged ART initiation or restart, HIV clinic referral, or CD4 count — recognizing that TB/HIV co-infection requires integrated care.

clinical_management.label: Arranged appropriate isolation
clinical_management.evidence: Student considered airborne precautions: isolation admission, mask, or referral to a dedicated TB facility.

clinical_management.label: Ordered appropriate investigations
clinical_management.evidence: Student ordered CXR, sputum GeneXpert (± culture), and HIV test to confirm the diagnosis and assess resistance.

interpersonal.label: Communicated with low-health-literacy patient
interpersonal.evidence: Used plain language, avoided medical jargon, checked understanding, and was not dismissive of the patient's "mine dust" theory.

interpersonal.label: Handled sensitive disclosure with empathy
interpersonal.evidence: When discussing HIV status and ART non-adherence, responded with empathy rather than judgment. Named the difficulty.

interpersonal.label: Explained public health rationale without stigmatising
interpersonal.evidence: Explained why isolation and contact tracing are needed in a way that did not blame or frighten the patient.

required_elements: Discussed default prevention — explained what happens if TB meds or ARVs are stopped
Advised return for worsening dyspnea, hemoptysis, or high fever
Explained contact tracing to the patient and offered to notify the hostel
